console.title: Voice Link
console.emptyIdle: Use the mic or type below to start.
console.hintIdle: Tap the mic for continuous listening, hold it (or Space) for one message. Esc stops playback.
console.hintHandsFree: Just speak — Jarvis listens between replies. Tap the mic or Esc to stop.
console.hintPtt: Release the mic or Space to send. Esc cancels.
console.hintBusy: Hold the mic or Space to interrupt with a new message. Esc silences Jarvis.
console.sttBlocked: Speech recognition is unavailable on this system, so the microphone is disabled. You can still type below.
console.speechUnsupported: Speech recognition is not available in this runtime — use the text field instead.
console.enableSttHint: For reliable voice input, enable local Whisper (free, offline) or add a cloud speech-to-text key in settings — typing always works.

quick.title: Quick actions
quick.capabilitiesPrompt: Introduce yourself briefly: what can you do for me from this app?
quick.briefPrompt: Give me a concise brief: anything important I should know or handle right now?
quick.planPrompt: Help me plan my day. Ask what is on my plate, then propose a short prioritized plan.

setup.title: Set up Jarvis Mode
setup.intro: Jarvis Mode is a hands-free voice console for the Hermes agent. It needs the Hermes CLI installed on this machine.
setup.step1Body: Install Hermes by Nous Research and make sure the command below is available in your terminal. Agent Club detects it automatically.
setup.step2Body: For spoken replies in Hermes's own voice, run this once in your terminal. Without it, Jarvis falls back to your system voice.
setup.step3Body: Talking to Jarvis uses the app's Speech to Text tool: choose Local Whisper (free, offline) or add a cloud key under Settings → Capabilities → MCP & Voice. The system asks for microphone permission on first use — and typing always works without any of this.
setup.stillOffline: Hermes was not detected yet. Install it, then re-scan.

control.installHint: Install Hermes to enable spoken computer control.
control.armedHint: Armed — spoken requests can drive this Mac. Disengage to disarm.
control.disarmedHint: Engage computer control to let spoken requests drive this Mac.
